name: Robin Harris
blurb: Robin Harris was an American comedian and actor best known for his raucous club act and his comic sketch featuring the unruly Bebes Kids, whose catchphrase was We dont die, we multiply.
bio: Robin Harris was an American comedian and actor best known for his raucous club act and his comic sketch featuring the unruly Bebes Kids, whose catchphrase was We dont die, we multiply. A fixture of the Comedy Act Theater in Los Angeles, he influenced a generation of Black comedians before his early death. He died in 1990 of cardiac arrhythmia at age 36, after which his sketches were adapted into the 1992 animated film Bebes Kids.

timeline.ev: Headlined the Comedy Act Theater in Los Angeles
Died of cardiac arrhythmia at age 36
Bebes Kids released posthumously as an animated film

accolades: Creator of the Bebes Kids comic characters
